channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: The barber for team Egypt hopes his haircuts are bringing World Cup luck
item.description: Egypt has never advanced this far in the World Cup. A Seattle-area barber hopes his haircuts for the team are bringing them luck.
item.content:encoded: Egypt has never advanced this far in the World Cup. A Seattle-area barber hopes his haircuts for the team are bringing them luck.
item.dc:creator: Freddy Monares

item.title: America 250: What's the vibe?
item.description: As America prepares to celebrate its 250th anniversary, are people excited and proud to celebrate? And how does it compare to the last major anniversary, the bicentennial in 1976?
item.content:encoded: As America prepares to celebrate its 250th anniversary, are people excited and proud to celebrate? And how does it compare to the last major anniversary, the bicentennial in 1976?
item.dc:creator: Frank Langfitt

item.title: Recovering the missing in Venezuela
item.description: Thousands remain missing more than a week after the twin earthquakes struck Venezuela, as the search for loved ones continues, even after nightfall.
item.content:encoded: Thousands remain missing more than a week after the twin earthquakes struck Venezuela, as the search for loved ones continues, even after nightfall.
item.dc:creator: Eyder Peralta

item.title: Runners in Atlanta prepare for a hot 10k race on the Fourth of July
item.description: Runners in Atlanta will be dealing with more heat during this year's Fourth of July Peachtree Road Race.
item.content:encoded: Runners in Atlanta will be dealing with more heat during this year's Fourth of July Peachtree Road Race.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Andrews

item.title: The effort to get benefits to American civilians exposed to toxic burn pits
item.description: A burn pit law from Congress left out thousands of American civilians working for the government who were exposed to the same toxins. Now there's an effort to change that.
item.content:encoded: A burn pit law from Congress left out thousands of American civilians working for the government who were exposed to the same toxins. Now there's an effort to change that.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas

item.title: June gloom for the job market as employers add just 57,000 jobs
item.description: U.S. employers added fewer jobs than expected last month. The unemployment rate fell, but only because a large number of people stopped looking for work.
item.content:encoded: U.S. employers added fewer jobs than expected last month. The unemployment rate fell, but only because a large number of people stopped looking for work.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: The Continental Congress wrote the Declaration. Is its modern descendent living up?
item.description: The Second Continental Congress produced the Declaration of Independence 250 years ago. Its legacy can also be seen in the Congress we know today. But is the legislative branch living up to the aspirations of that era?
item.content:encoded: The Second Continental Congress produced the Declaration of Independence 250 years ago. Its legacy can also be seen in the Congress we know today. But is the legislative branch living up to the aspirations of that era?
item.dc:creator: Sam Gringlas

item.title: When temperatures increase, mental health risks can too
item.description: Much of the country is reeling under a heat wave, which poses risks not just to people's physical health, but also mental health. We look at those risks and who is most likely to be affected.
item.content:encoded: Much of the country is reeling under a heat wave, which poses risks not just to people's physical health, but also mental health. We look at those risks and who is most likely to be affected.
item.dc:creator: Rhitu Chatterjee

item.title: Lobster may be on the menu for Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's NYC nuptuals
item.description: NPR's Juana Summers talks with Nicki Gostin of Page Six about the rumored wedding between Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce this weekend in New York City.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Juana Summers talks with Nicki Gostin of Page Six about the rumored wedding between Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce this weekend in New York City.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Bartlam

item.title: A hot summer trend in the sharing economy? Rental swimming pools
item.description: Don't have a pool during this hot summer? Rent one! Swimply is a platform that lets users rent private pools.
item.content:encoded: Don't have a pool during this hot summer? Rent one! Swimply is a platform that lets users rent private pools.
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha
